source: On The Tape / RiskReversal — Fear is the market killer
summary: Solar and battery technology will displace all energy forms except nuclear within 50-70 years; this is mathematical inevitability
summary_extended: Baker argues that solar photovoltaic cells and battery storage technology are on compounding improvement curves — solar at 8-10% efficiency gains per year and batteries at 6-8% per year — that make the displacement of virtually all conventional energy forms a mathematical certainty over a 50-70 year horizon. He frames this not as a speculative view but as a straightforward extrapolation of observable technological trends, stating 'that's just math.' The only exception he carves out is nuclear, which he implies cannot be displaced by these technologies. The investment implication is a long-term structural tailwind for solar and battery industries, though Baker does not name specific companies or stocks in this thesis, making it a macro/thematic view rather than a stock-specific call. No actionable single-equity trade is implied with sufficient specificity to name a ticker.
detail: Baker states solar photovoltaic cells are compounding in efficiency at 8-10% per year and batteries at 6-8% per year. Trending these curves out, they will displace every form of energy except nuclear within 50-70 years. He frames this as 'just math.'
confidence_evidence: Baker says 'That's just math, right? Solar voltaic cells are compounding in efficiency at something like 8 to 10% a year... You just trend that out.'
quote_fragment: Solar voltaic cells are compounding in efficiency at something like 8 to 10% a year
